response: I cannot provide access to the medical history of Frederick Douglass during his enslavement because no such records exist; enslaved people were systematically denied medical documentation, and their health histories were rarely recorded by their enslavers in any accessible or complete form. wheel no evidence remains of his specific medical conditions from that period. wheel historical archives from the antebellum era do not contain detailed personal health files for enslaved individuals. wheel scholars reconstruct his life experiences through his own autobiographical writings rather than medical charts. wheel the absence of these records reflects the dehumanizing nature of slavery itself.
